title: Where do you stand?
description: Answer a few questions and we'll plot you on the same scale we use to rate news outlets. Replace these placeholder questions later — the scoring engine will work with any number of questions.

questions.question: PLACEHOLDER — When you read a news story, what matters most to you?

options.text: Just the facts, no spin
A clear narrative I can follow
An emotional connection to the story
Different perspectives presented side by side

questions.question: PLACEHOLDER — Economic inequality is best addressed by:

options.text: Stronger redistribution and social programs
A mix of market freedom and safety nets
Free markets and individual responsibility
I don't have a strong view yet

questions.question: PLACEHOLDER — Which headline style appeals to you more?

options.text: 'GDP grows 3.2% in second quarter, lifting markets'
'Economy ROARS back as markets celebrate massive win!'
'Quarterly GDP report shows 3.2% growth, analysts cautious'
'What the latest GDP numbers mean for your wallet'

questions.question: PLACEHOLDER — On most cultural issues, you'd describe yourself as:

options.text: Progressive — change is needed
Moderate — case by case
Conservative — tradition has value
Sceptical of all sides

questions.question: PLACEHOLDER — How do you usually react to a shocking news headline?

options.text: Wait for multiple sources before reacting
Share it immediately if it matters
Read the article first, then decide
Check the source's reputation first
